---
title: "basics"
format: html
engine: julia
execute:
  daemon: false
julia:
  exeflags: ["--project=.@"]
---

# basics

## definiton

meta is a kind of automated operation of code, meaning the function of function.

## usage

###  Syntax-level tools: you should print the specific code to check.

```{julia}
# Syntax-level tools: you should print the specific code to check.
macro debug(expr)
    return quote
        val = $(esc(expr)) # print specific input instead of result.
        println("expr = ", $(string(expr)))
        println("value = ", val)
        val
    end
end

@debug 1 + 2
```

### Reducing repetition code

```{julia}
# Reducing repetition code
for (name, op) in [("add", :+), ("sub", :-), ("mul", :*)] # operation could be a input variable.
    fname = Symbol("my_", name) # @eval: generate code when run.
    @eval $fname(x, y) = $op(x, y) # define the function with the specific operation.
end

my_add(1, 1)
my_sub(1, 1)
my_mul(1, 1)
```

**In fact, this function is usually used in multiple dispatch**. 

```{julia}
struct A end
struct B end
struct C end

types = [:A, :B, :C]
values = [1, 2, 3]

for (T, v) in zip(types, values)
    @eval foo(x::$(T)) = $v
end
```
